title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has released a response to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its plans to address the committee's recommendations. This document likely provides details on the government's stance and strategies for implementing policies related to AI and public standards.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of copyright laws on the development of artificial intelligence systems that use existing copyrighted works. It examines the current issues surrounding AI and copyright.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The UK Department for Education is developing a policy on the use of generative AI in schools, which will guide how these tools can be used to support student learning. The policy aims to ensure that generative AI is used responsibly and effectively in education.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport Artificial Intelligence Action Plan is a document outlining how the Department for Transport (DfT) will partner with the transport sector to leverage the benefits of AI. The plan aims to achieve this while minimizing potential risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This policy document discusses copyright works in the development of AI systems. The report provides an update on the use of copyrighted material in AI creation under a specific section of an Act related to data access.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has developed a policy response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device. The policy aims to provide clear guidelines for the safe use and regulation of AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications to ensure fairness and integrity. It was written in response to a government request to provide clearer guidance on AI in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report by the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, outlining its policy on AI. The response is in direct reaction to the committee's findings and recommendations in their report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a review examining how artificial intelligence aligns with public standards. The review aims to ensure artificial intelligence is developed and used responsibly.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written information to a committee exploring how to regulate Artificial Intelligence.
